meta.title: OCF-XV manuscript pilot
meta.note: OpenAtlas-style pilot for OCF-XV. Current repositories are certain; origin/context places are parsed from existing metadata and marked by certainty.
meta.entityEnrichment: Actors and references enriched for OCF-XV.

ordines.label: Ratold Ordo
ordines.description: The most widely copied of all French coronation ordines. Named after Abbot Ratold of Corbie (d. 986). Of Anglo-Saxon origin, adapted for the Continent. Contains references to Albion and St. Gregory in the oldest manuscript. The ordo was consulted for French coronations probably from Louis VI's coronation in 1108 onwards. 20 surviving manuscripts (A-T) plus lost manuscripts.

manuscripts.label: A — Paris, BnF, MS lat. 12052
manuscripts.description: Sacramentary copied for Abbot Ratold. Oldest surviving ms. Base text of all editions.

manuscripts.label: B — Paris, BnF, MS lat. 13313
manuscripts.description: Primary witness of the Ratold ordo in this codex at the listed folios.

manuscripts.label: G — Reims, Bibl. mun., MS 342
manuscripts.date: Second half or end of 12th century
manuscripts.description: Key manuscript. Located in coronation cathedral. Source of MSS K, P, R, T. Marginal additions from English source, early 13th c. Possibly used at coronations of Philip Augustus (1179), Louis VIII (1223), or Louis IX (1226).

manuscripts.label: H — Sens, Bibl. mun., MS 9
manuscripts.description: Incomplete witness preserving part of the Ratold ordo.
